2603.24382.title: MolEvolve: LLM-Guided Evolutionary Search for Interpretable Molecular Optimization
2603.24382.paper_summary: Despite deep learning's success in chemistry, its impact is hindered by a lack of interpretability and an inability to resolve activity cliffs, where minor structural nuances trigger drastic property shifts. Current representation learning, bound by the similarity principle, often fails to capture these structural-activity discontinuities. To address this, we introduce MolEvolve, an evolutionary framework that reformulates molecular discovery as an autonomous, look-ahead planning problem. Unlike traditional methods that depend on human-engineered features or rigid prior knowledge, MolEvolve leverages a Large Language Model (LLM) to actively explore and evolve a library of executable chemical symbolic operations. By utilizing the LLM to cold start and an Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) engine for test-time planning with external tools (e.g. RDKit), the system self-discovers optimal trajectories autonomously. This process evolves transparent reasoning chains that translate complex structural transformations into actionable, human-readable chemical insights. Experimental results demonstrate that MolEvolve's autonomous search not only evolves transparent, human-readable chemical insights, but also outperforms baselines in both property prediction and molecule optimization tasks.
2603.24382.paper_summary_zh: 儘管深度學習在化學領域取得了成功，但其影響受到可解釋性不足和無法解決活動懸崖的限制，活動懸崖是指微小的結構差異會引發劇烈的性質變化。當前的表示學習受到相似性原則的限制，往往無法捕捉這些結構-活性不連續性。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了MolEvolve，一個將分子發現重新定義為自主的前瞻性規劃問題的進化框架。與依賴人為設計特徵或僵化先驗知識的傳統方法不同，MolEvolve利用大型語言模型（LLM）主動探索並進化一個可執行的化學符號操作庫。通過利用LLM進行冷啟動，以及使用蒙特卡羅樹搜索（MCTS）引擎進行測試時的規劃，並結合外部工具（例如RDKit），該系統能夠自主發現最佳軌跡。這一過程演變出透明的推理鏈，將複雜的結構轉變轉化為可行的、易於人類理解的化學見解。實驗結果顯示，MolEvolve的自主搜索不僅能夠演變出透明且易於人類理解的化學見解，還在性質預測和分子優化任務中超越了基準。
2603.24382.author: Xiangsen Chen et.al.
2603.24382.authors: Xiangsen Chen, Ruilong Wu, Yanyan Lan, Ting Ma, Yang Liu
